target.name: Kairos Power
target.founder: Mike Laufer, Edward Blandford, Per Peterson

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Late Stage → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $1.6B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Nuclear Energy. Range: $1.0B - $4.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: TerraPower
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Late Stage), post-money $4B+

data.narrative:
Kairos Power is a late-stage nuclear energy company that has raised over $500M and carries a current post-money valuation of $1.6B+. The peer universe comprises 10 comparable companies—3 public and 7 private—operating in the nuclear energy sector at similar valuation tiers, including TerraPower, X-Energy, Nano Nuclear Energy, and Zap Energy.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier to the $1.6B last post-money, implying $1.9B. Peer-median analysis across the 4-company comparable set yields a median valuation of $1.8B, with a range spanning $1.0B to $4.0B. The dispersion reflects meaningful heterogeneity in development stage and technology maturity across the peer group.

Triangulation of both methods supports a valuation range of $1.5B to $2.2B, with a median of $1.8B. This range sits modestly above the current $1.6B valuation, consistent with typical step-up dynamics in late-stage rounds. Key uncertainties include execution risk on commercialization timelines, regulatory approval pathways specific to Kairos's reactor design, and the maturity dispersion within the peer set—TerraPower at $4.0B versus Zap Energy at $1.0B—which reflects meaningful differences in technical de-risking and capital requirements.
